title: raylib TexturesReplay Bootstrap
summary: A hard two-phase bootstrap task. Phase A repairs the hollowed `rtextures.c` engine surface plus two `rcore.c` correctness bugs. Phase B builds a stronger deterministic replay harness that mixes CPU image pipelines, GPU texture roundtrips, and aggregate hashing.
instructionPreview: You are given: - A raylib source tree at /workspace/raylib - A deterministic headless mini-game project at /workspace/game_project (TexturesReplay) - Replay and expected-output assets under /workspace/assets - Helper scripts under /workspace/scripts
instruction:
You are given:
- A raylib source tree at /workspace/raylib
- A deterministic headless mini-game project at /workspace/game_project (TexturesReplay)
- Replay and expected-output assets under /workspace/assets
- Helper scripts under /workspace/scripts

TexturesReplay is a hard bootstrap task over raylib's textures stack.
It now exercises:
- procedural CPU image generation
- pixel-format conversion and internal pixel-storage helpers
- color conversion/blending utility functions
- in-place CPU image transforms and palette access
- CPU-side image drawing
- GPU texture upload, render-texture drawing, and screen readback
- a deterministic multi-tick replay harness that folds both CPU and GPU probes into a final hash

Your task is to complete a full bootstrap workflow:

Phase A - Engine repair and verification
1) Complete intentionally hollowed textures module implementation. Multiple
   function families are missing or broken at once: image I/O, generation,
   color utilities, pixel-format helpers, CPU transforms, CPU drawing, GPU
   texture objects, and the replay-facing harness glue. Validate behavior
   end-to-end instead of assuming a single local edit is enough.
2) Build raylib:
     /workspace/scripts/build_raylib.sh
3) Run focused engine checks:
     /workspace/scripts/run_engine_checks.sh

Phase B - Game repair and verification
4) Implement /workspace/game_project/replay_runner.c so replay execution produces
   correct outputs from real textures-module behavior.
5) Run the game twice with the same replay and seed:
     RUN_DIR=/workspace/output/replay_run_1 /workspace/scripts/run_headless_replay.sh
     RUN_DIR=/workspace/output/replay_run_2 /workspace/scripts/run_headless_replay.sh
6) Copy the first run as canonical game output:
     cp /workspace/output/replay_run_1/final_state.json /workspace/output/game_final_state.json
     cp /workspace/output/replay_run_1/final_state.sha256 /workspace/output/game_final_state.sha256
7) Write /workspace/output/determinism_report.json with:
     same_seed_hash_equal, run_1_hash, run_2_hash,
     run_1_event_lines, run_2_event_lines
     (derive hashes from your own replay outputs; no reference SHA256 is shipped in the workspace.)

Required output files:
- /workspace/output/engine_checks.json
- /workspace/output/engine_test_stdout.log
- /workspace/output/replay_run_1/final_state.json
- /workspace/output/replay_run_1/final_state.sha256
- /workspace/output/replay_run_1/texture_events.jsonl
- /workspace/output/replay_run_1/engine_trace.json
- /workspace/output/replay_run_2/final_state.json
- /workspace/output/replay_run_2/final_state.sha256
- /workspace/output/replay_run_2/texture_events.jsonl
- /workspace/output/replay_run_2/engine_trace.json
- /workspace/output/game_final_state.json
- /workspace/output/game_final_state.sha256
- /workspace/output/determinism_report.json

Rules:
- Do not edit files under /tests.
- Do not fabricate hashes; compute them from the actual JSON outputs you generate.
- Do not bypass engine build/check scripts.
- Do not replace textures-module logic with mocks or stubs.

moduleDag.description: A hard two-phase bootstrap task. Phase A repairs the hollowed `rtextures.c` engine surface plus two `rcore.c` correctness bugs. Phase B builds a stronger deterministic replay harness that mixes CPU image pipelines, GPU texture roundtrips, and aggregate hashing.

nodes.label: Engine Probe Runner
nodes.description: Adds the dedicated engine probe binary, its build target, and the script wrapper that materializes `engine_checks.json` from real engine behavior.

nodes.label: rcore.c Correctness Fixes
nodes.description: Fixes the incorrect `GetScreenHeight()` field access and the inclusive `GetRandomValue()` range bug.

nodes.label: Procedural Image Generation
nodes.description: Restores generated solid, gradient, checked, noise, and related CPU image constructors used by both the engine probe and replay harness.

nodes.label: Color Math Utilities
nodes.description: Restores color conversion, alpha, blending, lerp, HSV, normalization, and packing helpers used directly by the harder replay logic and indirectly by CPU/GPU drawing routines.

nodes.label: Pixel-Format Conversion & Pixel Storage Helpers
nodes.description: Restores image copy/crop/format conversion plus the internal and public pixel-storage helpers (`GetPixelColor`, `SetPixelColor`, `GetPixelDataSize`, `HalfToFloat`, `FloatToHalf`, `LoadImageDataNormalized`) they depend on.

nodes.label: Image File I/O
nodes.description: Restores image load/export paths from file, memory, raw buffers, texture readback, and screen readback.

nodes.label: Image Transforms & Color Ops
nodes.description: Restores resize, canvas resize, alpha ops, blur/kernel/mipmap/dither, flips/rotations, and in-place per-channel image color mutations.

nodes.label: CPU Image Drawing Primitives
nodes.description: Restores pixel/line/circle/rectangle/triangle drawing into CPU images plus image-to-image blitting.

nodes.label: GPU Texture Objects & Draw Paths
nodes.description: Restores texture creation/update/mipmap/filter/wrap APIs, render textures, validity checks, and the `DrawTexture*` family used by the hard replay.

nodes.label: TexturesReplay Harness
nodes.description: Implements the deterministic multi-tick replay harness that combines CPU image generation, format conversion, color utilities, transforms, palette/pixel access, CPU drawing, GPU upload/draw/readback, and final aggregate hashing.

edges.label: Per-unit validation for later engine nodes uses the engine probe runner.
Per-unit validation for later engine nodes uses the engine probe runner.

Per-unit validation for later engine nodes uses the engine probe runner.
Per-unit validation for later engine nodes uses the engine probe runner.
Image I/O validation uses generated images for file/memory roundtrips.
`LoadImageRaw` and texture readback rely on correct pixel-size and format helper behavior.
Image color transforms depend on shared color math helpers.
Image transforms depend on core format/pixel-storage helpers.
Pixel/palette access relies on correct pixel decode helpers and format conversion.
Image drawing uses color blending helpers.
Image drawing relies on pixel encode/decode helpers and format-aware paths.
Texture creation from file/memory depends on image I/O.
Texture upload and cubemap helpers depend on pixel-format conversion.
Replay creates multiple procedural images every tick.
Replay derives tint/blend/HSV colors from shared color helpers.
Replay copies, crops, reformats, and samples CPU image buffers.
Replay reads back texture and screen images.
Replay applies deterministic image transforms and color ops every tick.
Replay aggregates palette and pixel-level metrics every tick.
Replay draws into CPU canvases before GPU upload.
Replay uploads CPU canvases to textures, renders them, and samples the result.
Replay uses seeded RNG and records screen dimensions in the final state.
